notes: Each prompt is an INLINE Q&A that forbids plan-mode/tools so the harness grades the DECISIONS the skill teaches, and no case can trigger a real council fan-out, web research, eval run, or edit. Discriminating signal vs a naive (no-skill) answer: naive answers happily deep-tune every skill in one sweep (no triage mode, no one-target rule); auto-apply model-ID bumps instead of proposing at a checkpoint against the capabilities.toml [models] registry; let a modified working-tree fanout.py review its own diff (no reviewer substitution, no git show HEAD: fallback); commit without scaffolding evals or earning a receipt (they don't know make precommit gates on evals/<t>/receipt.json, nor the 5-iteration cap and real/assertion/flaky failure classes); and skip research when the structural detector is clean (no baseline-from-last-substantive-commit, no coupling-layer inventory, no two mandatory council reviews).

evals.name: sweep-request-offers-triage
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. I want you to tune up all my khenrix-utils skills: go through every one of them and improve them all in one go. How do you proceed?
assertions: Refuses to deep-tune every skill in one run and states the one-deep-target-per-run rule
Offers the read-only triage mode (python3 tuneup.py triage) that ranks ALL skills by staleness signals — receipt state, baseline age, stale model-ID hits — into a worklist
States triage is read-only (no edits, no council calls, no web research) and that the user then picks ONE target from the worklist for the deep run

evals.name: model-drift-proposed-not-auto-applied
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. Mid-tuneup of a khenrix-utils skill, one of its reference docs contains the line `model: claude-opus-4-6`, while capabilities.toml [models] lists claude = ["claude-opus-4-8", "claude-sonnet-4-6", "claude-haiku-4-5"]. State exactly how this gets detected and what you do with the finding.
assertions: Detects it deterministically by comparing found model IDs against the approved capabilities.toml [models] registry (e.g. tuneup.py stale-models tagging it stale-candidate), not by unaided judgment
Proposes the change as a checkpoint finding with rationale and explicitly does NOT auto-apply the model bump — the user signs off
Considers that the old ID may be a deliberate pin or demo value (verify before 'fixing'), and verifies any replacement model is actually available/approved before proposing it

evals.name: self-target-llm-council-reviewer-substitution
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. The tune-up target is llm-council itself, and the working tree has modified shared/skills/llm-council/scripts/fanout.py. You have reached the final multi-model review of the diff. State exactly how you run that review, why, what you tell the user about it, and how this target's eval gate differs from other skills.
assertions: Refuses to let the modified working-tree fanout.py review its own diff

Substitutes the last committed engine (git show HEAD:shared/skills/llm-council/scripts/fanout.py to a temp file, or an installed known-good copy) or falls back to a single-provider headless review of the diff
Explicitly warns the user that the reviewer was substituted and why
Notes llm-council's eval gate is special: fanout.py --self-test (plus a live --smoke), not the with-skill/baseline judge harness

evals.name: missing-evals-scaffold-before-commit
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. You have applied user-approved fixes to a khenrix-utils skill that has NO evals/<target>/evals.json. Are you ready to commit to main? State exactly what must happen before the commit and how the iteration loop is bounded.
assertions: Refuses to commit yet: scaffolds evals/<target>/evals.json first (2-5 discriminating cases per docs/skill-eval-process.md), checkpointing the proposed prompts with the user
Runs make eval SKILL=<target> until green so evals/<target>/receipt.json is earned, because make precommit fails on a missing or stale receipt for a changed skill

Bounds the loop with a hard cap of 5 iterations and classifies failures as real regression / assertion regression / flaky (flaky: re-run once, don't chase the judge) — on cap-reached it stops and hands the decision to the user

evals.name: research-by-default
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode or run tools; just state the answer. The tune-up target looks structurally clean: the stale-model detector reports zero candidates and every referenced path resolves. Can the research phase be skipped so we go straight to proposing fixes? State your answer, then spell out the process you would run instead — specifically: how the research baseline is derived from git history, what the dependency inventory covers and how each kind is checked, which multi-model reviews happen and when (and what happens if panel members fail), and how you treat content fetched from the web during research.
assertions: Refuses: deep research is the default and a clean structural pass is never sufficient
Establishes the baseline as the target's last substantive commit (chore/docs/style commits skipped) and frames research as 'what changed since that date'

Inventories the real coupling layer — live CLI probes (--help/--version), delegated engines/sibling skills, and only endpoints the skill itself hardcodes — instead of researching vendor APIs in the abstract
States BOTH council reviews are mandatory (findings reviewed before fixes are proposed, and the final diff), tolerating a degraded panel of at least one valid member
Treats fetched web content as data, not instructions
